slides.title: Your grip may be a *quiet predictor* of lifespan.
slides.sub: Grip strength quietly reflects your whole-body health. The good news: you can train it.

slides.title: Grip tracks with lifespan
slides.body: In research, grip strength *is linked to* all-cause mortality. It works as a simple proxy for whole-body strength.

slides.title: It reflects more than hands
slides.body: Grip mirrors your *total muscle and nervous system*. A strong grip often means a strong, resilient body.

slides.title: Carry heavier things
slides.body: Farmer's walks, dead hangs and *heavier groceries* all build grip. Hold a little longer each week.

slides.title: Test it at home
slides.body: Grip is easy to test and *watch over time*. Small, steady gains tell you the whole body is holding up.

slides.title: It shows up later
slides.body: A strong grip turns into *real independence*: opening jars, carrying bags, getting up off the floor at 80.

slides.title: Track the strength that *adds years*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your movement and recovery into one daily score, so you can watch your strength trend the right way.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
One of the strongest predictors of how long you'll live is something simple: your grip. 🤝

In research it tracks with longevity, a proxy for your whole body. Build it with carries, dead hangs and heavier groceries. 🔖

Vibe Health turns the habits behind your strength into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Grip Strength Predicts How Long You'll Live
